RootCauses.Evidence: Baseline repository shape loaded all matching FabricRoll rows into memory, then aggregated/projected in C#. The optimized page shape fetches only the requested rows.
RootCauses.Solution: Implemented server-side pagination and DTO projection, composite/partial FabricRoll indexes, and grouped warehouse aggregates.

RootCauses.Evidence: Baseline stock balance shape loaded warehouse_stocks and all rolls, then repeatedly filtered rolls per stock row in memory.
RootCauses.Solution: Implemented SQL GROUP BY roll-cost aggregation and joined projected stock balances.

RootCauses.Evidence: The mobile page issues warehouses/dashboard/alerts calls. Backend aggregation is the main cost now; on 3G payload and latency add visible delay.
RootCauses.Solution: Response compression is enabled and a paged rolls API contract is available for future roll-list screens.

Questions.desktop_rows_displayed: The legacy GetFabricRollsAsync now returns a capped first page for compatibility; GetFabricRollsPageAsync exposes the explicit server page contract.
Questions.wpf_virtualization: WPF design was not changed; the UI service now has a paged method so screens can bind pages without loading tens of thousands of rows.
Questions.filter_location: Inventory filtering in tested repository methods is server-side only for simple WHERE clauses, but aggregation and many projections run client-side in C#.
Questions.ef_loading: No lazy-loading proxy was found; slowness is from explicit ToListAsync full loads and follow-up dictionary/lookups.
Questions.sql_executed: EXPLAIN ANALYZE output is included in the JSON report.
Questions.web_api_calls_inventory_page: 3 calls in web-client Inventory.tsx: warehouses, dashboard, alerts.
Questions.web_parallel_or_sequential: React Query calls are declared independently and can run in parallel.
Questions.web_pagination: A paged web API endpoint is available at GET /api/v1/inventory/warehouses/{warehouseId}/rolls with pageNumber/pageSize/status/search.
Questions.json_response_size: Measured in payloadBytes per web measurement in the JSON report.
Questions.fabric_roll_indexes: The model now defines WarehouseId/Status, WarehouseId/FabricColorId, ContainerId/Status, Status, and available-roll partial indexes.
Questions.partial_index_complete: The partial index uses the actual enum value Status = 0 for available rolls and RemainingLengthMeters > 0.
Questions.columns_returned: The paged roll path projects DTO fields instead of materializing full FabricRoll entities.
Questions.includes: No large Include chain was found in the inventory repository; related data is loaded with separate lookup queries.
Questions.n_plus_one: Warehouse list now uses grouped stock and roll-value queries instead of per-warehouse stock/roll queries.
Questions.lookup_cache: Account and fabric catalog lookups now use in-memory cache with prefix invalidation on writes.
